summary: Loan Application Service — REST API and DTO Layer
description:
Implement the complete REST API layer for the loan-service and establish the shared loanflow-common Maven module as a minimal DTO library. This epic builds on the completed data layer and covers all four Loan Application Service endpoints defined in the API reference: create application, get application by ID, search applications, and update application status.

All request and response DTOs are placed in loanflow-common so other services can depend on a single shared contract library. The loanflow-common module already exists and is parented by loanflow-parent, which in turn inherits spring-boot-starter-parent. Validation and Lombok dependencies are therefore version-managed by the parent chain — loanflow-common must only declare what it strictly needs as a DTO library and must not pull in web, actuator, resilience4j, or other runtime concerns.

The service layer is scaffolded as an interface plus a stub implementation where each method returns a new response instance populated with placeholder values matching the API reference — this ensures the application context loads, endpoints are addressable, and response shapes can be verified end-to-end before business logic is added.
userType: backend developer owning the Loan Application Service
functionality: implement the full REST API layer for loan application submission, retrieval, search, and status management, with request validation, RBAC enforcement, and shared DTOs in a lean loanflow-common module
benefit: so the loan-service exposes a complete, validated, role-protected API surface that the frontend and gateway can consume, and so loanflow-common stays a lean DTO library with no unnecessary transitive dependencies introduced to consuming services

stories.summary: Trim loanflow-common to minimal DTO dependencies and add shared domain types
stories.description:
The existing loanflow-common/pom.xml pulls in spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-starter-actuator, and resilience4j, none of which belong in a DTO library. These inflate the classpath of every service that declares loanflow-common as a dependency.

This story trims the pom to the minimum needed for annotation-only DTOs: spring-boot-starter-validation (for Bean Validation annotations), lombok, and jackson-databind (already available via spring-boot-starter-parent but declared explicitly for clarity). All version management is inherited from loanflow-parent, so no versions are specified inline.

After trimming, this story adds the shared domain enums and the generic PagedResponse wrapper that all loan application DTOs depend on.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: loanflow-common builds without web or actuator on the classpath
acceptanceCriteria.given: the loanflow-common pom contains only spring-boot-starter-validation, lombok, and jackson-databind
acceptanceCriteria.when: mvnw clean package is run from backend/loanflow-common
acceptanceCriteria.then: the module compiles and produces a plain JAR without errors and no spring-boot repackage goal runs

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: loan-service imports loanflow-common types without pulling in web artifacts twice
acceptanceCriteria.given: loanflow-common is a dependency of loan-service and the trimmed pom is in place
acceptanceCriteria.when: mvn dependency:tree is run on loan-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: spring-boot-starter-web and spring-boot-starter-actuator appear only once, sourced from loan-service, not duplicated from loanflow-common

acceptanceCriteria.given: a PagedResponse<ApplicationSummaryItem> is constructed with content, page, size, totalElements, and totalPages
acceptanceCriteria.then: all five fields appear at the top level with the correct types and the generic content array is present

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Domain enums are available in loanflow-common without JPA annotations
acceptanceCriteria.given: LoanType, LoanStatus, and EmploymentStatus exist under com.loanflow.common.enums
acceptanceCriteria.when: a class in loan-service imports these enums
acceptanceCriteria.then: the imports resolve without error and no @Enumerated or jakarta.persistence annotations are present on the enum classes

tasks.summary: Trim loanflow-common pom.xml to DTO-only dependencies
tasks.description:
Edit backend/loanflow-common/pom.xml. Remove the following dependencies that have no place in a DTO library:

- spring-boot-starter (no auto-configuration needed)
- spring-boot-starter-web (web runtime belongs in service modules)
- spring-boot-starter-actuator (operational endpoint belongs in service modules)
- resilience4j-core, resilience4j-retry, resilience4j-circuitbreaker (resilience belongs in service modules)

Retain or add only the following. All versions are managed by the loanflow-parent → spring-boot-starter-parent chain, so no version attributes are needed:

- spring-boot-starter-validation (provides jakarta.validation annotations for @NotNull, @NotBlank, @Valid, etc. on DTOs)
- lombok (optional, annotation processor only)
- jackson-databind (for @JsonInclude, @JsonProperty on response types)

Verify the trimmed pom still compiles cleanly with mvnw clean package.

tasks.summary: Create shared domain enums under com.loanflow.common.enums

stories.summary: Create Loan Application request DTOs in loanflow-common
stories.description: Implement the request DTO classes for the two write endpoints: CreateLoanApplicationRequest (POST /api/v1/applications) and UpdateApplicationStatusRequest (PATCH /api/v1/applications/{applicationId}/status). Both DTOs live under com.loanflow.common.dto.application.request and carry Bean Validation annotations so the controller can apply @Valid and delegate all constraint checking to the framework. No validation logic is written by hand.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: CreateLoanApplicationRequest fails validation when loanType is null
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationRequest is built with a null loanType
acceptanceCriteria.when: Bean Validation is applied via @Valid in the controller
acceptanceCriteria.then: a MethodArgumentNotValidException is raised identifying the loanType field

acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationRequest contains an ApplicantDetailsRequest with email set to 'not-an-email'
acceptanceCriteria.then: a constraint violation is raised on the email field

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: UpdateApplicationStatusRequest fails validation when status is null
acceptanceCriteria.given: an UpdateApplicationStatusRequest is built with a null status
acceptanceCriteria.then: a MethodArgumentNotValidException is raised identifying the status field

acceptanceCriteria.given: a fully populated CreateLoanApplicationRequest with valid loanType, amount within range, termMonths within range, positive annualIncome, employmentStatus, and a valid applicantDetails block

stories.summary: Create Loan Application response DTOs in loanflow-common
stories.description: Implement all four response DTO classes for the Loan Application Service endpoints. Response DTOs are read-only projections of entity state assembled by the service layer. All classes live under com.loanflow.common.dto.application.response in loanflow-common. Field names and shapes match the API reference exactly so Jackson serialisation produces the documented contract without additional configuration.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: CreateLoanApplicationResponse serialises to the documented POST shape
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationResponse is built with applicationId, status SUBMITTED, submittedAt, loanType, amount, and nextStep UPLOAD_REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON matches the API reference shape for POST /api/v1/applications with all six fields present

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ApplicationDetailResponse includes nested applicantDetails and documentSummary
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ApplicationDetailResponse is built with a nested ApplicantDetailsSummary and DocumentSummary
acceptanceCriteria.then: applicantDetails and documentSummary appear as nested objects with their documented fields

acceptanceCriteria.given: an ApplicationSearchResponse is built with a list of ApplicationSummaryItem objects and pagination metadata
acceptanceCriteria.when: it is returned from the controller and serialised
acceptanceCriteria.then: content, page, size, totalElements, and totalPages all appear at the top level

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: UpdateApplicationStatusResponse reflects both previousStatus and currentStatus
acceptanceCriteria.given: an UpdateApplicationStatusResponse is built with previousStatus SUBMITTED and currentStatus PENDING_DOCUMENTS
acceptanceCriteria.then: both status fields appear as string enum values alongside updatedAt and reason

tasks.summary: Create ApplicationDetailResponse DTO with nested types

stories.summary: Implement LoanApplicationController, service interface, and stub service layer
stories.description:
Implement LoanApplicationController in loan-service, the LoanApplicationService interface it depends on, and a stub LoanApplicationServiceImpl where each method returns a new response instance populated with placeholder values that match the API reference example responses. This approach loads the application context, makes all four endpoints addressable, and allows response shapes to be verified before real business logic is added.

Also includes the GlobalExceptionHandler that converts validation and domain exceptions into the standard error payload, and the SecurityConfig that activates JWT resource server validation and @PreAuthorize method security.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications is restricted to APPLICANT role
acceptanceCriteria.given: a request is sent to POST /api/v1/applications with a JWT carrying role LOAN_OFFICER
acceptanceCriteria.when: the request reaches the security filter chain
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 403 Forbidden response is returned and the controller method body is not executed

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications returns 400 on validation failure with standard error payload
acceptanceCriteria.given: a CreateLoanApplicationRequest is submitted with a null loanType
acceptanceCriteria.then: GlobalExceptionHandler intercepts MethodArgumentNotValidException and returns a 400 response with timestamp, status, error, message, and path fields

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/applications returns a stub CreateLoanApplicationResponse with status SUBMITTED
acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid CreateLoanApplicationRequest is submitted with an APPLICANT JWT
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 201 Created response is returned with a CreateLoanApplicationResponse body containing applicationId, status SUBMITTED, submittedAt, loanType, amount, and nextStep

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications is restricted to LOAN_OFFICER and ADMIN roles
acceptanceCriteria.given: a request is sent to GET /api/v1/applications with a JWT carrying role APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.when: the request reaches the security filter chain

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications/{applicationId} returns 404 when application does not exist
acceptanceCriteria.given: the stub service throws ResourceNotFoundException for an unknown applicationId
acceptanceCriteria.then: GlobalExceptionHandler returns a 404 response with the standard error payload

tasks.summary: Create LoanApplicationServiceImpl stub returning placeholder response instances
Create GlobalExceptionHandler with standard error payload
tasks.description:
Create com.loanflow.loanservice.exception.GlobalExceptionHandler in loan-service annotated with @RestControllerAdvice.

First define the error payload record:

public record ApiErrorResponse(
    Instant timestamp,
    int status,
    String error,
    String message,
    String path
) {}

Then handle the following exception types:

1. MethodArgumentNotValidException → 400 Bad Request
   Collect all field violation messages into a comma-separated string for the message field.

2. ResourceNotFoundException → 404 Not Found

3. AccessDeniedException (org.springframework.security) → 403 Forbidden

4. ResponseStatusException → pass through the embedded status code and reason

5. Exception (catch-all) → 500 Internal Server Error with message 'An unexpected error occurred'

All handlers build an ApiErrorResponse. Source the path field from HttpServletRequest.getRequestURI().

tasks.summary: Create LoanApplicationController with all four endpoints
Add SecurityConfig with JWT resource server and @EnableMethodSecurity
Verify context loads and all four endpoints respond correctly via dev profile
tasks.description:
Start loan-service with the dev profile and confirm:

1. Spring context loads without errors.
2. GET /actuator/health returns 200.
3. POST /api/v1/applications without a JWT returns 401.
4. POST /api/v1/applications with an APPLICANT JWT and an invalid body (null loanType) returns 400 with the ApiErrorResponse payload.
5. POST /api/v1/applications with an APPLICANT JWT and a valid body returns 201 with a CreateLoanApplicationResponse containing status SUBMITTED.
6. GET /api/v1/applications with an APPLICANT JWT returns 403.
7. GET /api/v1/applications with a LOAN_OFFICER JWT returns 200 with an empty ApplicationSearchResponse.
8. PATCH /api/v1/applications/1/status with a LOAN_OFFICER JWT and a valid body returns 200 with an UpdateApplicationStatusResponse.
